전도서 10:1.content: Dead flies cause the oil of the perfumer to produce an evil odor; so does a little folly outweigh wisdom and honor.

전도서 10:2.content: A wise man’s heart is at his right hand, but a fool’s heart at his left.

전도서 10:3.content: Yes also when the fool walks by the way, his understanding fails him, and he says to everyone that he is a fool.

전도서 10:4.content: If the spirit of the ruler rises up against you, don’t leave your place; for gentleness lays great offenses to rest.

전도서 10:5.content: There is an evil which I have seen under the sun, the sort of error which proceeds from the ruler.

전도서 10:6.content: Folly is set in great dignity, and the rich sit in a low place.

전도서 10:7.content: I have seen servants on horses, and princes walking like servants on the earth.

전도서 10:8.content: He who digs a pit may fall into it; and whoever breaks through a wall may be bitten by a snake.

전도서 10:9.content: Whoever carves out stones may be injured by them. Whoever splits wood may be endangered by it.

전도서 10:10.content: If the ax is blunt, and one doesn’t sharpen the edge, then he must use more strength; but skill brings success.

전도서 10:11.content: If the snake bites before it is charmed, then is there no profit for the charmer’s tongue.

전도서 10:12.content: The words of a wise man’s mouth are gracious; but a fool is swallowed by his own lips.

전도서 10:13.content: The beginning of the words of his mouth is foolishness; and the end of his talk is mischievous madness.

전도서 10:14.content: A fool also multiplies words. Man doesn’t know what will be; and that which will be after him, who can tell him?

전도서 10:15.content: The labor of fools wearies every one of them; for he doesn’t know how to go to the city.

전도서 10:16.content: Woe to you, land, when your king is a child, and your princes eat in the morning!

전도서 10:17.content: Happy are you, land, when your king is the son of nobles, and your princes eat in due season, for strength, and not for drunkenness!

전도서 10:18.content: By slothfulness the roof sinks in; and through idleness of the hands the house leaks.

전도서 10:19.content: A feast is made for laughter, and wine makes the life glad; and money is the answer for all things.

전도서 10:20.content: Don’t curse the king, no, not in your thoughts; and don’t curse the rich in your bedroom: for a bird of the sky may carry your voice, and that which has wings may tell the matter.
